docs.title: 1. Executive Summary
docs.text: VisionStudio is a desktop application that provides an LLM-powered workspace for authoring, evaluating, and iterating on product specifications using the VisionSpec methodology. It combines structured spec workflows with AI-assisted writing and LLM-as-a-Judge evaluation to help product teams create high-quality specifications efficiently.

docs.title: Current State
docs.text:
Product teams creating specifications face several challenges:

  1. Fragmented tooling - Specs live in Google Docs, Notion, Confluence with no workflow awareness
  2. Quality inconsistency - No objective evaluation of spec completeness or quality
  3. Manual iteration - Teams manually review specs without structured feedback
  4. Methodology drift - Teams start with a methodology (Working Backwards, Shape Up) but abandon rigor under time pressure
  5. Context switching - Writing specs requires jumping between editor, research, and review

docs.title: Pain Points
docs.text:
• "I wrote a PRD but I'm not sure if it's good enough"
  • "We started using Working Backwards but stopped doing the FAQ"
  • "I have to copy-paste between my spec and ChatGPT to get feedback"
  • "Our specs are inconsistent across teams"

docs.title: Primary: Product Managers
docs.text:
• Write MRDs, PRDs, and coordinate spec workflows
  • Need structured guidance on methodology
  • Want objective quality feedback before stakeholder review

docs.title: Secondary: Technical Writers / Technical PMs
docs.text:
• Author TRDs and technical specifications
  • Need templates and evaluation for technical completeness
  • Want to ensure alignment with PRD requirements

docs.title: Tertiary: Engineering Leads
docs.text:
• Review and approve TRDs
  • Need visibility into spec status and quality
  • Want to trace technical decisions to product requirements

docs.title: Existing Solutions
docs.text: Solution Gap Google Docs / Notion No workflow, no evaluation, no LLM integration ChatGPT / Claude.ai No structure, no persistence, no methodology Linear / Jira Task tracking, not spec authoring Coda / Airtable Flexible but no spec-specific workflows

docs.title: Why Now
docs.text:
• LLMs capable of meaningful evaluation (Claude 3.5+, GPT-4o+)
  • Teams adopting AI for writing assistance
  • Remote work increased need for clear written specs
  • VisionSpec library provides foundation (profiles, rubrics, synthesis)

docs.title: OKRs
docs.text:
Objective: Establish VisionStudio as the standard for AI-assisted spec authoring

  • KR1: 100 active users within 6 months of launch
  • KR2: 80% of users complete full workflow (MRD → TRD) at least once
  • KR3: Average spec evaluation score improves 20% from first to final draft
  • KR4: NPS > 50 among active users

docs.title: Success Metrics
docs.text: Metric Target Rationale Specs completed per user/month > 2 Demonstrates regular usage Evaluation-to-revision rate > 60% Users act on LLM feedback Profile adoption > 3 profiles used Validates methodology library value Session duration 15-45 min Focused work sessions

docs.title: Core Capabilities
docs.text:
1. Project Management
  2. Create/manage multiple spec projects
  3. Each project is a directory with structured spec files

  4. Projects can be product-level (MRD start) or feature-level (OpportunitySpec start)

  5. Profile-Driven Workflows

  6. Select from profiles: aws-product, aws-feature, big-tech-product, big-tech-feature, etc.
  7. Visual workflow diagram showing spec sequence and dependencies

  8. Status indicators: not started, draft, evaluated, approved

  9. Spec Authoring

  10. Markdown editor with profile-specific templates
  11. Toggle between Source (Markdown) and Rendered view

  12. Template variables auto-populated from project context

  13. LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation

  14. Evaluate spec against profile rubric
  15. Detailed scoring by category (e.g., Problem Clarity, User Stories, Technical Completeness)

  16. Actionable feedback with specific improvement suggestions

  17. LLM Writing Assistant

  18. Chat panel for spec assistance
  19. Context-aware: knows current spec, project, profile

  20. Can draft sections, improve clarity, expand details

  21. Synthesis

  22. Auto-generate downstream specs from source specs
  23. Example: Generate Press Release from MRD
  24. Human review and edit before finalization

docs.title: Information Architecture
docs.text:
Left Panel (Navigation + LLM)
├── Project selector
├── Profile dropdown
├── Workflow link (opens diagram)
└── Spec list (ordered by workflow)
    ├── MRD ✓ (9.2)
    ├── Press Release ✓ (8.5)
    ├── FAQ ⚠ (6.1)
    └── ...
─────────────────────────────
LLM Chat Panel
├── Message input
└── Conversation history

Main Panel (Content)
├── Workflow diagram (when Workflow selected)
└── Spec editor (when spec selected)
    ├── Toolbar: [Source | Rendered]
    └── Content area

docs.title: Key Interactions
docs.text:
1. Start new project → Choose profile → Project created with empty specs
  2. Open spec → See template if empty, content if exists
  3. Edit spec → Autosave, Markdown editing
  4. Evaluate → Click evaluate or ask LLM → See scores and feedback
  5. Act on feedback → Edit spec → Re-evaluate → Iterate until passing
  6. Synthesize next → When spec passes, synthesize downstream specs

docs.title: Must Have
docs.text:
• Offline-capable - Core editing works without internet
  • Local-first - Specs stored as local Markdown files
  • Cross-platform - macOS, Windows, Linux
  • LLM-agnostic - Support Claude, GPT, local models via omniagent

docs.title: Architecture Decisions
docs.text:
• Desktop app built with Wails (Go + TypeScript/React)
  • Import visionspec for profiles, templates, rubrics, evaluation
  • Import omniagent for LLM interface
  • Specs stored in user's filesystem, not cloud

docs.title: 9. Risks and Mitigations
docs.text: Risk Impact Mitigation LLM evaluation quality varies Users lose trust Use structured rubrics, show reasoning, allow override Users don't complete workflows Low engagement Gamification (progress indicators), guided prompts Too complex for casual users Low adoption Sensible defaults, progressive disclosure Wails platform issues Release delays Test on all platforms early, have Electron fallback

docs.title: 10. Non-Goals (Out of Scope)
docs.text:
• Cloud sync / collaboration (future phase)
  • Mobile app
  • Integration with Jira/Linear (future phase)
  • Custom rubric editor (use visionspec CLI)
  • Multi-language support

docs.title: 11. Open Questions
docs.text:
1. Should we support custom profiles created in the app, or only visionspec-provided profiles?
  2. How do we handle LLM API key management securely?
  3. Should evaluation history be persisted for trend analysis?
  4. Do we need a "team" concept, or is this purely single-user initially?
